sources.ref: Notes from chat with interaction design.canvas

behaviours.detail: Canvas: 'Minimal journey is select type e.g. animals, or plants.' The obligations-v2 model already supports a degenerate journey of one section, one page, one obligation - no extension needed.

Canvas: 'Multiple pages in a task (linear journey in a task). Sub tasks within tasks as well.' Sections-with-pages covers the first half, but the model has no nesting below section - sub-tasks within tasks would need a third level. | Ruling (spec gate 2026-07-07): flow/page-layer concern answered by the built journey (sections+pages), not an obligation-model fact.

Canvas: 'Back button implementation - back as in journey, back as in to hub, back as in to Check Your Answers.' The model has no navigation-context vocabulary; each page's back target must be resolved from context rather than a hardcoded destination. | Ruling (spec gate 2026-07-07): the spike's navigation model answers back semantics; not a model-level fact.

Canvas: 'Delete conditional data on change of determining condition, e.g. delete crash records when saying you didnt have any claims.' Matches the model's wipeOnExit semantics - the spec defaults wipeOnExit=true on every activatedBy obligation (retain cases must be argued at a gate; see conflict c-017).

Canvas: 'Current design is linear followed by task list.' The model orders sections and pages but has no presentation-mode concept distinguishing a linear opening run from a task-list hub - and the canvas itself asks what determines linear vs task list. | Ruling (spec gate 2026-07-07): flow/page-layer concern; the built journey decides presentation, the model only orders sections.

Canvas: '*Some* questions are mandatory at the point of display - cannot Save and Continue without entering a valid value.' V4 distinguishes Mandatory to proceed from Mandatory to submit, but the spec's mandate vocabulary collapses both to required:true (mandateRaw preserves the distinction verbatim). A distinct at-display mandate may be a model extension. | Ruling (spec gate 2026-07-07): mandate is two-level - enforcedAt continue vs submit. V4 'Mandatory to submit' rows carry enforcedAt=submit; which fields (if any) are continue-level is open as c-023.

Canvas: 'More than one page / question can lead to the same end field. Does this mean one obligation, two implementations? Or logical OR on obligations.' Directly contradicts the boot-guard coverage rule (every obligation collected by exactly one page); unresolved on the canvas too. | Ruling (spec gate 2026-07-07): closed - not a real case for this journey; no one-obligation-collected-by-many-pages support needed.

Canvas: 'Multiple Journeys - and one journey might navigate to another (how about multiple drivers on our car insurance).' The spec models a single journey; cross-journey navigation and per-entity journey repetition are beyond the current model. | Ruling (spec gate 2026-07-07): closed - not a real requirement for this journey.

Canvas: 'Field level values persisted on page.' The skeleton's save model is page-owned spine (every POST rebuilds and saves the whole notification); persisting individual field values without a page submit is not something the current model or skeleton does. | Ruling (spec gate 2026-07-07): requirement is only that committed values reappear when returning to a page; the page-owned save model already provides this. No sub-page/keystroke persistence needed.

Canvas: 'Partial page completion is supported, even if a field is required for the overall outcome.' Matches the model's split between page save and submission-level mandate enforcement - required:true obligations gate submission, not page save (V4's Mandatory to submit).

Canvas: 'Questions can be in scope but optional - e.g. bring your own label/reference number.' Matches the model's empty mandate {} - used for internalReferenceNumber, numberOfPackages, transitedCountries and the individual animal identifier fields.

Canvas: 'Task list where some pages navigate to the next task, rather than back to task list.' Per-page exit-navigation configuration is not in the model; the skeleton exhibits exactly this ambiguity (cph-number short-circuits the addresses hub to /port-of-entry, conflict c-019). | Ruling (spec gate 2026-07-07): flow-layer navigation choice per page; not a model-level fact.

Canvas: 'Navigation can be determined by state of journey.' Matches the model's activatedBy/gate semantics - conditional pages (import-purpose, cph-number, transporter spokes) appear or disappear based on earlier answers.

Canvas: 'Configuration can affect obligations - e.g. versioning, feature flags, A/B testing, statute change.' The spec's obligation set is static; resolving the active set against configuration (version, flag, A/B arm, statute change) is a model extension. | Gate note (2026-07-07): deliberately deferred - complicated; revisit at a later gate, not blocking the build.

Canvas open question: 'JavaScript in Browser?' - whether client-side JavaScript can be assumed or required is unresolved. Bears on the documents upload loop (scan-status polling) and any conditional-reveal widgets. | Ruling (spec gate 2026-07-07): not a model question. Requirement is graceful degradation (works without client-side JS, progressively enhanced) - the skeleton journey already demonstrates this.

Canvas open question: 'What to do about conditional data after a rule change? Might be constrained by the delete conditional data steer.' Interacts with configuration-affects-obligations and wipeOnExit.

Canvas open question: 'What determines it being linear vs task list?' - the canvas does not say what property of a journey decides linear presentation versus a task list. Pairs with the linear-then-task-list behaviour. | Ruling (spec gate 2026-07-07): flow-layer; determined when the journey is built, not specced at model level.

Canvas open question: 'Save by copy, by reference, or both? E.g. address example.' The spec's address fieldGroup usages assume copy semantics by default; recorded as conflict c-020 against all six address-block obligations. | Ruling (spec gate 2026-07-07): save by copy. Matches the spec's address fieldGroup copy semantics.

address.detail: V4 Standard Address Block (#address_block): Name or Organisation name (string max 255, Mandatory); Address Line 1 (string max 255, Mandatory); Address Line 2 (string max 255, Optional); Town or city (string max 100, Mandatory); County (string max 100, Optional); Postal or Zip code (string max 12, Mandatory); Country (enum from MDM country list, Mandatory); Telephone number (string max 20, Mandatory); Email address (string max 254, Mandatory). The parent field determines whether an address record is required; this validation applies once the record is provided. Copy semantics assumed for all usages (see conflict c-020).

pages.title: Import notification service
pages.notes: Paginated, sortable list of existing notifications; POST /start-journey resets the whole session then redirects to origin. Also hosts copy and delete actions (notification-copy / notification-delete).

pages.title: Origin of the import
pages.notes: First step; successful POST saves the draft and captures the backend-assigned referenceNumber into session. regionOfOriginCode added here as a conditional reveal (skeleton never collected it - c-012).

pages.title: Select a commodity
pages.notes: Entry page for the commodityLines collection - member fields (commoditySelection, typeSelection, speciesSelection, numberOfPackages, numberOfAnimalsQuantity, animalIdentifiers) are covered via the collection's item[].

pages.title: Select species of commodity
pages.notes: Collects typeSelection and speciesSelection for the current commodity line (covered via commodityLines.item). Shows a read-only commodity-details table; preserves per-species values on reselect (c-017).

pages.title: Description of goods
pages.notes: Collects numberOfAnimalsQuantity and numberOfPackages for the current commodity line (covered via commodityLines.item). Skeleton renders one row per selected species and recomputes derived subtotals (c-003).

pages.title: Enter animal identification details
pages.notes: Entry surface for the nested animalIdentifiers unit collection (covered via commodityLines.item -> animalIdentifiers.item). Skeleton shows one table per selected species with ear tag and passport inputs only (c-003).

pages.title: Reason for import
pages.notes: Skeleton heading: What is the main reason for importing the animals? Sits at skeleton step 4, interleaved mid-commodity (c-019); grouped here under the skeleton's About the consignment page group.

pages.title: Purpose in the internal market
pages.notes: Invented page (no skeleton counterpart, c-012): conditional follow-up shown when reasonForImport = Internal market.

pages.title: Additional animal details
pages.notes: Skeleton template page group: Description of the goods. containsUnweanedAnimals only shows for the five listed commodity groups (cross-frame gate, modelGap).

pages.title: Accompanying documents
pages.notes: Entry page for the documents collection (upload loop: add/remove one document per POST; Continue enabled only when no scan is PENDING or REJECTED). Item fields covered via documents.item.

pages.title: Addresses
pages.notes: Hub page: renders sections for place of origin, consignor/exporter, consignee, importer, place of destination and CPH with Add/Change links to the select spokes; collects nothing itself. Skeleton hub POST swallows save failures (c-006) and three of its spokes are dead (c-005).

pages.title: Search for an existing consignor or exporter
pages.notes: Spoke of the addresses hub; skeleton selection is a GET link back to the hub with an index into mock-consignors.json.

pages.title: Search for a place of destination
pages.notes: Spoke of the addresses hub; skeleton selection is a GET link back to the hub with an index into mock-destinations.json.

pages.title: Search for a place of origin
pages.notes: Invented page: the skeleton hub links to /place-of-origin/select but the route is unregistered (dead spoke, c-005).

pages.title: Search for a consignee
pages.notes: Invented page: the skeleton hub links to /consignees/select but the route is unregistered (dead spoke, c-005).

pages.title: Search for an importer
pages.notes: Invented page: the skeleton hub links to /importers/select but the route is unregistered (dead spoke, c-005).

pages.title: Add the County Parish Holding number (CPH)
pages.notes: Presented as an addresses-hub spoke but the skeleton POST jumps straight to /port-of-entry, short-circuiting the hub (c-019). Only shown when a CPH-required commodity is selected (cross-frame gate, modelGap).

pages.title: Entry point and arrival at destination
pages.notes: Skeleton template page group: Transport. Title keeps the skeleton's arrival-at-destination wording, but V4 defines arrival date AT PORT (c-011) - copy to reconcile with Figma.

pages.title: How the animals will travel
pages.notes: Invented page (no skeleton counterpart, c-012) for the four V4 transport fields; transitedCountries reveals only for Railway/Road Vehicle.

pages.title: Transporter
pages.notes: Hub page. Skeleton has a single mock transporter select with no type split; V4 requires choosing Commercial vs Private first (c-012), so this page collects transporterType and hands off to the matching spoke.

pages.title: Search for an existing transporter
pages.notes: Spoke of the transporters hub; maps to V4 Commercial Transporter (consumed from the TBC approved-transporter list; address block + approval number). Skeleton options come from load-mock-transporters.js.

pages.title: Private transporter details
pages.notes: Invented page (no skeleton counterpart, c-012): standard address block entry shown when transporterType = Private transporter.

pages.title: Contact address for consignment
pages.notes: Last data-entry step before the CYA. Registered inside the addresses plugin but reached in-flow from the transporters POST (c-019). gov.identity variant: if more than one address exists the user must select one before continuing; user-created variant uses the standard address block (c-001).

pages.title: Notification details
pages.notes: CYA hub: re-fetches the notification from the backend and re-hydrates every session key; summary cards with Change links to each step; Confirm and submit only when status is DRAFT.

pages.title: Declaration
pages.notes: POST validates the confirmation checkbox then calls the submit endpoint (DRAFT to SUBMITTED) and redirects back to /declaration - no separate confirmation page (c-022).

obligations.label: Reference Number
obligations.notes: System-assigned by the backend on first successful save (origin POST). Format GBN-AG-YY-XXXXXX: GBN = Great Britain Notification, AG = Animals and Germinals, YY = two-digit allocation year, body = six-character Crockford base32 non-guessable identifier. Displayed as a caption on every subsequent page and used in /notification-view/{referenceNumber}.

obligations.label: Responsible Person for Load
obligations.notes: Consumed from gov identity on authentication - not collected by any page. Composite of six sub-fields: Responsible person, Telephone, Email, Organisation Name, Organisation Address, Organisation Telephone.

input.valuesSource: Named MDM list of EU, EEA and EFTA countries (skeleton uses reference-data countries client, group GBNAG_SPS_EX, with blank placeholder and disabled divider row)

obligations.label: Region of origin code requirement

input.hint: If a region of origin code is required it will be shown on your health certificate.

obligations.notes: Skeleton (requiresRegionCode) defaults to no and accepts an empty answer; V4 makes it mandatory to submit - V4 wins (see c-006).

obligations.label: Region of origin code
obligations.notes: Displayed and stored with ISO country prefix derived from Country of Origin. Not collected anywhere in the skeleton despite requiresRegionCode existing (see c-012).

obligations.label: Internal reference number

input.hint: Enter any internal reference number you want to use to identify this consignment, or leave blank.

obligations.notes: Optional trader-entered reference for the consignment. V4 carries an unresolved inline comment on the Mandatory cell ('Optional'). NB: V4 example Imports456_GB contains an underscore, which the skeleton's alphanumeric-only pattern would reject.

obligations.label: Animals certified for

values: Live aquatic animals for human consumption
input.hint: You will find this on the health certificate.

obligations.notes: PO approval pending: APHA intend to make this conditional for some commodities - pending confirmation. Unresolved inline comment on field name.

obligations.label: Contains Unweaned Animals

input.hint: These are animals that are still feeding from their mother.

obligations.notes: Notification-level field gated on commodity selections made inside the commodityLines collection - the reverse of the proposed frame:enclosing direction; frame:anyItem is proposed vocabulary meaning shown when any commodity line matches. PO approval pending.

obligations.label: Reason for import
obligations.notes: V4 carries an unresolved inline comment on 'Temporary admission horses'.

obligations.label: Purpose in internal market

values: Companion animal not for resale or rehoming

obligations.notes: Not present in the skeleton at all (see c-012); collected on an invented provisional page.

obligations.label: Place of Origin
obligations.notes: Standard address block; user must provide one address record. Dead spoke in the skeleton: hub link to /place-of-origin/select is unregistered and session key placeOfOrigin is never written (see c-005).

obligations.label: Consignor
obligations.notes: Standard address block; user must provide one address record. Skeleton selects a whole address object from mock-consignors.json via the /consignors/select spoke and sends it as notification.consignor.

obligations.label: Consignee
obligations.notes: Standard address block; user must provide one address record. Dead spoke in the skeleton: /consignees/select is unregistered and session key consignee is never written (see c-005).

obligations.label: Importer
obligations.notes: Standard address block; user must provide one address record. Dead spoke in the skeleton: /importers/select is unregistered and session key importer is never written (see c-005).

obligations.label: Place of destination
obligations.notes: Standard address block; user must provide one address record. Skeleton selects a whole address object from mock-destinations.json via the /destinations/select spoke and sends it as notification.destination.

obligations.label: Contact Address
obligations.notes: Two V4 variants share one anchor (see c-001): consumed from gov.identity ('if more than one address exists, the user must select one before continuing') or created by the user via the Standard address block. Modelled as one select-or-create obligation. Skeleton: radios over mock-contacts.json indexes, stored as consignmentContactAddress and sent as notification.consignment.contact; a separate sessionKeys.contactAddress is defined but unused.

obligations.label: Transporter type
obligations.notes: User must select one transporter type. Skeleton has no type split - a single mock transporter select (see c-012).

obligations.label: Commercial Transporter
obligations.notes: Standard address block PLUS approval number; single entry for the consignment. Consumed from a TBC list of approved transporters (unresolved inline comment on that clause); if a trader creates a new commercial transporter it is restricted to NI.

obligations.label: Private Transporter
obligations.notes: Standard address block; single entry for the consignment. Not present in the skeleton (see c-012); collected on an invented provisional page.

obligations.label: County Parish Holding (CPH)

includes: 01051500 - Poultry - Day old chicks - Guinea Fowl

obligations.notes: Single entry for the notification, required when CPH applies to the selected commodity (19 listed commodities) - notification-level field gated on commodityLines contents (frame:anyItem proposed vocabulary). Unresolved inline comment on field name.

input.valuesSource: Named MDM live animal ports of entry list (skeleton hardcodes ABERDEEN / EAST MIDLANDS AIRPORT / EDINBURGH)

obligations.label: Arrival date at Port

input.validation: Must be a valid date in DD/MM/YYYY format

obligations.notes: Unresolved inline comment on field name. Skeleton asks for arrival at final destination with lax validation (see c-011).

obligations.label: Means of transport
obligations.notes: Not present in the skeleton (see c-012); collected on an invented provisional page.

obligations.label: Transport identification
obligations.notes: Free-text transport identifier. Not present in the skeleton (see c-012).

obligations.label: Transport document reference
obligations.notes: Free-text transport document reference. Not present in the skeleton (see c-012).

obligations.label: Transited Countries
obligations.notes: V4 tension recorded verbatim: Conditions cell says 'Conditional: required when Means of transport is Railway or Road Vehicle. Optional multi-select.' while the Mandatory cell says Optional - both clauses carry unresolved inline comments. Modelled as optional but only shown for Railway/Road Vehicle. Not present in the skeleton (see c-012).

obligations.mandateRaw: Required to submit (skeleton Joi: must equal confirmed)
obligations.label: I declare that the information I have provided in this notification I am submitting is true and correct.
obligations.notes: Skeleton-only field: not persisted; a valid POST calls POST /notifications/{referenceNumber}/submit (DRAFT to SUBMITTED) and redirects back to /declaration with no separate confirmation page. Shows Date of declaration as today (en-GB).

obligations.label: Commodity lines
obligations.notes: V4 data-level model: a commodity code plus its taxonomy chain (Type, Class, Species) plus a quantity; things true at the specific commodity line level. Skeleton supports exactly one line (commodityComplement single-element array) - see c-003.

provenance.ref: table:Live Animal Data Elements (data-level model prose)

obligations.mandateRaw: Field Block - Mandatory to Submit - At least one
obligations.label: Animal identifiers (unit records)
obligations.notes: V4 Level 3: Unit record - 0..N per commodity line, carrying per-animal identifiers and per-animal Permanent Address. Banner rule: a notification must be submitted with at least one animal identifier PER ANIMAL - the at-least-one applies across the sibling identifier fields within each unit record, not just to the item count (hence modelGap sibling-at-least-one). Each identifier field is individually Optional.

obligations.label: Accompanying documents
obligations.notes: A notification can be submitted without an accompanying document, but once a document type is selected the attachment, reference and date of issue are mandatory to proceed (all-or-nothing per item - item fields carry required:true within an item). Skeleton mechanics: upload loop with per-document remove, max 10 documents, 10 MB cap, virus-scan polling (REJECTED must be removed before Continue); documents never enter buildNotificationPayload (see c-004).

obligations.label: Commodity selection

input.valuesSource: Named MDM commodity list; search supports common name, species (scientific name) and commodity code

obligations.notes: Unresolved inline comment on field name. Skeleton hardcodes Cow/Fish/Cat/Dog (see c-018).

obligations.label: Type selection

input.valuesSource: MDM; where applicable for given commodity the user can filter species by type

obligations.notes: Applicability is data-driven from MDM ('where applicable for given commodity') - not expressible as an activatedBy equals/includes rule, so no activatedBy is wired. Unresolved inline comment on the Example cell (Game). Skeleton offers only Domestic from mock-species.json and posts the type text, not its numeric value (see c-018).

obligations.label: Species selection

input.valuesSource: MDM species for the selected commodity/type
input.example: Bison bison; Bos spp.; Bos taurus; Bubalus bubalis

obligations.notes: User may select one or more species up to the agreed maximum (maximum not stated in V4). Skeleton stores each selection as {value, text} from mock-species.json and carries per-species values over on reselect (see c-017).

obligations.label: Number of packages

includes: 01063980 - Game Birds - Day old chicks - Partridge
01063980 - Game Birds - Day old chicks - Pheasant
04071919 - Game Birds - Hatching eggs - Pheasant and Partridge
01051500 - Poultry - Day old chicks - Guinea Fowl
01063200 - Psittaciformes (including parrots, parakeets, macaws and cockatoos)

obligations.notes: Shown where package count applies to the selected commodity (54-entry list, same commodity-line frame so activatedBy is natively expressible). List includes 0203 Pig Meat, a product (see c-021). Unresolved inline comments on field name and on 0203. Skeleton models this per species with derived subtotals (see c-003).

obligations.label: Number of animals (quantity)
obligations.notes: Unresolved inline comments on field name and on the int64 max in the Type cell; PO approval pending. Skeleton collects this per species and derives a subtotal (see c-003).

obligations.label: Animal Identifier - Passport
obligations.notes: Shown when the enclosing commodity line requires Passport identifier - unit-level field gated on the enclosing frame (proposed frame:enclosing vocabulary). V4 bracketed count says (6) but lists 9 entries (see c-002). PO approved with note: field condition pending review by Monica; unresolved inline comment on Mandatory cell.

obligations.label: Animal Identifier - Tattoo
obligations.notes: Shown when the enclosing commodity line requires Tattoo identifier (frame:enclosing). V4 bracketed count says (2) but lists 5 entries (see c-002). Field condition pending review by Monica; unresolved inline comment on Mandatory cell.

obligations.label: Animal Identifier - Ear Tag
obligations.notes: Shown when the enclosing commodity line requires Ear Tag (frame:enclosing); the (4) bracketed count matches its list. Field condition pending review by Monica; unresolved inline comment on Mandatory cell.

obligations.label: Horse Name
obligations.notes: Shown when the enclosing commodity line requires horse name (0101 - Horse only; frame:enclosing). No example in V4 and the PO Approved cell is empty (no task item). Unresolved inline comment on Mandatory cell.

obligations.label: Animal Identifier - Identification details
obligations.notes: Shown when the enclosing commodity line does NOT have a specific identifier type - a negated cross-frame condition with no explicit commodity list, so no activatedBy can be wired (modelGap). Captures free-text identification details. Field condition pending review by Monica; unresolved inline comment on Mandatory cell.

obligations.label: Animal Identifier - Description

input.example: Worker bees in sealed transport hive

obligations.notes: Shown when the enclosing commodity line does NOT have a specific identifier type - negated cross-frame condition with no explicit list, so no activatedBy can be wired (modelGap). Captures a free-text animal description. Field condition pending review by Monica; unresolved inline comment on Mandatory cell.

obligations.label: Permanent Address
obligations.notes: Required per animal when the enclosing commodity line is Cats, Dogs or Ferrets (frame:enclosing); a different address can be entered for each animal, so it lives on the unit record alongside the identifiers (V4 Level 3 prose). Standard address block. Unresolved inline comment on field name. Not present in the skeleton (see c-012).

obligations.label: Accompanying Document: Type
obligations.notes: Required within a document item (all-or-nothing block). PO approved with note Pending; unresolved inline comments on field name and Mandatory cell. Skeleton enum has only ITAHC and Veterinary health certificate (see c-010).

obligations.label: Accompanying Document: Attachment Type
obligations.notes: Required within a document item (all-or-nothing block). Skeleton implements this as an actual file upload instead - allowed extensions pdf/doc/docx/jpeg/jpg/png/xls/xlsx, max 10 MB (decimal, under the CDP nginx 10 MiB cap), uploaded to the document service and virus-scanned (see c-010). Unresolved inline comments on field name and Mandatory cell.

obligations.label: Accompanying Document: Reference
obligations.notes: Required within a document item (all-or-nothing block). Skeleton has it optional with max 100 and alphanumeric-only pattern (see c-010). Unresolved inline comments on field name and Mandatory cell.

obligations.label: Accompanying Document: Date of Issue

input.validation: Must be a valid date in DD/MM/YYYY format

obligations.notes: Required within a document item (all-or-nothing block). Skeleton requires it only when a file is attached, with day/month/year completeness and real-calendar-date checks, persisted per document as yyyy-mm-dd (see c-010). Unresolved inline comment on Mandatory cell.
